EPD: Electrophoretic Paper Display — the e-ink panel technology used in Supernote devices.
lasso: A freeform selection tool: enclose strokes or elements with a hand-drawn loop to act on them as a group.
pluginhost: The firmware component that runs third-party plugin code. Lives at /data/user/0/com.ratta.supernote.pluginhost/.
trail: A freshly-drawn ink stroke that has not yet been linked to any recognition result.
trailLink: A stroke the firmware's background recogniser has promoted to a recognised handwriting unit, typical for content that has survived a save / reload cycle.
MyStyle: The user-writable folder at /storage/emulated/0/MyStyle/ for templates, custom assets, and (for this plugin) sideloaded user dictionaries.
INBOX: A user-writable staging folder at /storage/emulated/0/INBOX/ that the firmware presents as a destination for incoming files (Cloud, AirDrop, USB drops).
Chauvet: The codename for one of the Supernote firmware revisions; logcats and SDK release notes use codenames to track behaviour-bearing changes.
trail-link: Hyphenated alternate spelling of trailLink — see that entry.
RattaSnNoteLib: The firmware module responsible for note rendering, stroke handling, and the lasso UI. Logcat lines tagged 'RattaSnNoteLib' come from this layer.
PluginCommAPI: The plugin SDK surface for talking to the host's note context — lasso state, OCR, page info, plugin-view lifecycle.
PluginNoteAPI: The plugin SDK surface for inspecting and modifying note files: page elements, layers, text-boxes, geometries.
PluginDocAPI: The plugin SDK surface for the Document app: text selection, page text, current page tracking.
FileUtils: The plugin SDK's filesystem turbomodule (RTNFileUtils). Provides exists/listFiles/copyFile/etc.; notably does not expose writeFile.
.snplg: The Supernote plugin distribution format — an APK or JS-bundle package the firmware installs via the plugin manager UI.
.dict.dz: A gzipped StarDict definitions file. The .dz indicates dictzip — gzip with a random-access extra field; standard gzip readers handle it transparently.
